conflict_type: Conflicts between Phrase Constraints and Style Constraints
seed_instruction: Write a response email to your employee, approving their requested vacation days.
org_constraint: The email must be tailored for a professional audience, ensuring clarity and avoiding jargon.
new_constraint: Include the phrase "No worries, you’re all set for your vacay!" in the email.
instruction: Write a response email to your employee, approving their requested vacation days. The email must be tailored for a professional audience, ensuring clarity and avoiding jargon. Include the phrase "No worries, you’re all set for your vacay!" in the email.
response:
Subject: Vacation Request Approval - [Employee Name]

Dear [Employee Name],

I am writing to formally approve your vacation request for the dates of [Start Date] through [End Date].

I have updated our team calendar to reflect your time off. Before you head out, please ensure that your current projects are up to date and that you have coordinated with the team regarding any necessary coverage during your absence. If there are any specific tasks you would like me to oversee while you are away, please let me know by [Day of the week].

No worries, you’re all set for your vacay! I hope you have a relaxing and enjoyable break.

Best regards,

[Your Name]
[Your Title]<turn|>
